title: SU West Campus One-Way to Two-Way Street Conversion -- Safety and Traffic Study
summary: Extensive discussion of Syracuse University's request to convert Stadium Place and Standard Street from one-way to two-way traffic. Neil Burke (DPW) clarifies SU initiated the request. Jennifer Bbeby (SU Campus Planning) provides I-81/dome shuttle context -- streets were two-way until 1979, switched to one-way for dome shuttle that no longer uses the route. Traffic engineer Amy Dake (Pasro Associates) presents safety case: two-way streets reduce speeds, improve pedestrian visibility, and are safer for cyclists. Council scrutinizes pedestrian safety near dormitories, congestion concerns during I-81 construction, and alignment with Vision Zero. Area not in high injury network. SU offers to absorb signage/restriping costs. Streets have already operated as two-way during recent dome events.

political_signal.description: Council members probe pedestrian safety, Vision Zero alignment, and cost responsibility for SU-requested street conversion

processing_notes: Streets (Stadium Place, Standard Street) originally two-way, converted to one-way in 1979 for Carrier Dome shuttle traffic. I-81 construction (Fine View/Renwick closures) adds urgency. Council Vision Zero questions satisfied -- area not in high injury network. SU to cover signage/restriping costs. Law Department detection is false positive from 'law' in pedestrian crossing law discussion. Syracuse Fire Department detection is from general mention of city departments supporting the change.
Segment 4 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
